mnemonics.title: Primary Pollutants记忆
mnemonics.content: S O N C O - Sulfur, Oxides, Nitrogen, Carbon, Others (remember as 'SONCO' sounds like sunny)

acronyms.usage: Measures how clean or polluted the air is (0-500 scale)
Tiny particles suspended in air, PM2.5 and PM10 are key measurements
Group of gases causing smog and respiratory problems
Carbon-based chemicals that evaporate easily and form smog
Synthetic chemicals that deplete ozone layer
International body providing air quality guidelines

visualTips.visualization: Picture a city with factory chimneys (stationary), cars (mobile), and homes (residential) all releasing different colored smoke streams into the sky
Imagine Earth wrapped in a blanket - greenhouse gases trap heat like glass in a greenhouse, preventing escape into space
Draw SO₂ and NOₓ rising from factories, reacting with clouds (water), and falling as acidic rain damaging trees and buildings

visualTips.visualize: Think of the ozone layer as Earth's sunscreen - CFCs act as holes in this sunscreen, allowing harmful UV rays to burn through

analogies.analogy: Like dust mites too small to see but everywhere - they float in the air and get into your lungs like water seeping through a coffee filter
Like a lid on a boiling pot - normally heat rises and mixes, but inversion traps pollution under a 'lid' of warm air
Like a chemical transformer station - takes dangerous raw materials (toxins) and converts them into safe outputs
Earth's natural sunscreen - just as sunscreen protects skin from UV burn, ozone protects all life from harmful radiation
